text: The loop measures each metric (baseline and per-experiment) exactly once, so a single noisy sample can be KEPT and permanently raise `best`, after which genuine later gains are rejected against an inflated bar. The generator should support repeated measurement: a `metric.repeat: N` config field that makes the generated baseline+eval worker prompts run the metric N times and report a median (plus spread), and a variance-aware keep decision so a within-noise move is not kept. Discriminating check (RED on current generator, GREEN after): generate a loop and assert (a) the eval worker prompt instructs running the metric N times and reporting a median/spread when `metric.repeat>1`, and (b) an exported pure helper takes a noise/spread argument such that an improvement smaller than the observed spread returns false. Both absent today.

On plateau the loop hard-stops (`shouldStop` returns 'plateau'), surrendering remaining budget at the first local optimum, and it only ever proposes from the single global `best`. Add a bounded escape: before a terminal stop, restart proposing from a non-incumbent (the baseline, or a kept-but-not-best commit) for K rounds. Discriminating check (RED now, GREEN after): unit-test a pure decision helper (an extended `shouldStop` or a new `nextAction(...)`) that, given a plateau with restart rounds remaining, returns a 'restart' signal instead of terminal 'plateau'; and assert the generated loop contains the restart branch that re-seeds the propose step from a non-incumbent. Today plateau is terminal-only and propose always seeds from `best`.

`budgets.total.wallclockMin` and `budgets.total.tokens` are offered in config but never fire on a fresh single-session run: the conductor reads `elapsedMin`/`tokensUsed` only from launch `args`, which are undefined until a resume, so `shouldStop` cannot trip them live. The checkpoint/eval workers already run every experiment and can return elapsed seconds and tokens; accumulate those and feed them into the next `shouldStop`. Discriminating check (RED now, GREEN after): assert the generated loop captures the worker-returned elapsed/tokens and passes accumulated LIVE values (not just `args.elapsedMin`/`args.tokensUsed`) into the subsequent `shouldStop` call inside the experiment loop. Today only the static `args.*` values reach `shouldStop`.

The propose agent receives only a thin ledger digest `{g, change, metric, kept}` with no failure REASON (gate-fail vs metric-regression vs surface-violation) and no diff, so it can re-propose the spirit of an already-failed idea. Record a discard-reason enum per experiment in the ledger and surface it in the propose digest. Discriminating check (RED now, GREEN after): assert (a) the ledger entry object the conductor pushes carries a `reason` field populated on every discard, and (b) the propose prompt's `ledgerDigest` map includes that reason field. Both absent today.

HUMAN-ONLY (no honest deterministic check — asserting prose would be a brittle grep): SKILL.md (line ~87) and references/metric-gate.md (line ~27) advertise wallclockMin/tokens budgets without noting they do not bound a fresh run. If `live-wallclock-token-budget` lands this is moot; otherwise the docs must state the limitation. Handle by hand or fold into `live-wallclock-token-budget`.
